reforms.description: The Swiss government's revitalisation package includes a complete revision of legislation against anti-competitive practices to increase competitive pressures and align commercial laws with the EU. Additionally, it proposes modifications to patent law to strengthen research in Switzerland and eliminate disadvantages faced by Swiss industry. A second wave of microeconomic reforms aims to relax the Post Office's monopoly in telecommunications and review construction norms to reduce expenses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance competition and productivity, which are expected to support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of legislation and economic policy aimed at revitalising the Swiss economy.
reforms.source_quote: In the context of the 'revitalisation programme', the Federal Council proposes to Parliament a modification to patent law aimed at strengthening research in Switzerland and abolishing the comparative disadvantages of Swiss industry in this domain.

reforms.description: The federal 'investment bonus programme' allocates SF 200 million for subsidies of 15 to 20 percent of the cost of additional infrastructure investment undertaken by municipalities and cantons, aimed at stabilizing construction activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme is designed to stimulate construction activity, which can have positive effects on the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been implemented and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in infrastructure but is not a major overhaul of policy.
reforms.source_quote: The federal 'investment bonus programme' devotes SF 200 million for federal subsidies of 15 to 20 per cent of the cost of additional infrastructure investment undertaken by municipalities and cantons.

reforms.description: A reform is being considered to mutual recognition of water connection permits, allowing firms to install fittings in local authority areas outside their own, enhancing competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing competition, the reform is expected to support the growth of innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently under consideration and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is a minor adjustment aimed at improving competition rather than a major policy change.
reforms.source_quote: A reform which will be particularly important is the mutual recognition of water connection permits.
